novel_features.name: Snapshot-aware playlist diff
novel_features.description: Compare a playlist's current state against any prior snapshot to see exactly which tracks were added, removed, or reordered.
novel_features.rationale: Spotify versions playlists with a snapshot_id but the UI exposes nothing — competitors don't snapshot, so they can't diff. The local store keeps every snapshot.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when a user asks 'what changed in this playlist?' or wants to undo an algorithmic refresh on a collab playlist.

novel_features.name: ISRC-aware playlist dedupe
novel_features.description: Find duplicate tracks in a playlist by ISRC (catches the album/single/EP/deluxe-reissue dupe class), report by default, --apply to remove.
novel_features.rationale: Track-ID dedupe misses reissue duplicates; ISRC is the recording-level identifier that catches them. The local store joins playlist_tracks to tracks.isrc.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when a curator's playlist has the same recording on three different albums; Spotify's app treats them as distinct.

novel_features.name: Cross-playlist merge with dedupe
novel_features.description: Combine multiple playlists into one with built-in dedupe and ordering controls.
novel_features.rationale: Spotify ships no merge primitive — users currently script this against spotipy. Local dedupe happens before the POST so the destination is clean from the first write.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when a DJ wants to consolidate retired set playlists into a deep-archive without manually de-duplicating.

novel_features.name: Top-tracks rotation drift
novel_features.description: Compare two top-tracks snapshots and show who rose, fell, or stayed stable across a time window.
novel_features.rationale: Spotify's top-tracks endpoint returns current only — without local snapshotting there is no drift answer. spotify-pp-cli records every top-tracks pull so this query becomes possible.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when a user asks 'which artists fell off my top-50 between Q1 and Q4?' or wants to track their own listening identity over time.

novel_features.name: Release-Radar replacement
novel_features.description: List new albums and singles released since a date by artists you follow, sorted newest first.
novel_features.rationale: Spotify deprecated the algorithmic Release Radar surface for new apps on 2024-11-27. This rebuilds the same answer deterministically by iterating followed_artists locally.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when a user asks 'what's new from artists I follow' and the deprecated Release Radar is no longer reachable.

novel_features.name: Cross-entity track lookup
novel_features.description: For a given track, find every place it appears in your data: which playlists, whether saved, last played, and on which devices.
novel_features.rationale: Cross-entity join across playlist_tracks, saved_tracks, play_history, and top_tracks_snapshot — pure local SQL. Spotify's app has no equivalent view.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this before adding a track to a playlist to avoid duping it, or to answer 'have I played this before' questions.

novel_features.name: Play-history by listening context
novel_features.description: Bucket your recent play history by the playlist or album that drove each play, ranked by play count and total duration.
novel_features.rationale: Spotify's API exposes context_uri per play but no UI aggregates it. The local play_history table (extended past the API's 50-item cap by frequent syncs) makes the aggregation possible.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when a DJ asks 'which of my set lists is actually getting played this week' or when a journalist wants column data on listening patterns.

novel_features.name: Agent-friendly queue-from-saved
novel_features.description: Pick N tracks from your saved library (optionally filtered by artist or playlist origin) and queue them in one command.
novel_features.rationale: Spotify Premium's /me/player/queue is one-track-per-call. Reading from local saved_tracks lets the agent batch the loop and filter without hammering the search endpoint.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when an agent should 'queue something from my chillout saved tracks' or 'queue 10 more from this artist' without resorting to URI manipulation.

novel_features.name: Genre-walked artist discovery
novel_features.description: Find artists you don't follow yet who match the genres of your top, saved, or followed artists, ranked by popularity within each genre.
novel_features.rationale: Spotify deprecated /recommendations for new apps on 2024-11-27. This rebuilds the 'artists you might like' question using /search?type=artist&q=genre:"..." which still works, seeded from the genres your existing favorites already wear.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when the user asks 'find me new artists like the ones I already listen to' and the deprecated recommendations endpoint isn't an option.

novel_features.name: Co-occurrence discovery via public playlists
novel_features.description: Find artists frequently co-curated with a seed artist by searching public playlists that contain them and ranking other artists by co-occurrence count.
novel_features.rationale: Spotify's /artists/{id}/related-artists is deprecated for new apps. Public playlists become the graph substrate — if hundreds of curators put artist A alongside artist B, B is a recommendation.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this for the 'who sounds like X' question Spotify used to answer with related-artists; curator-driven co-occurrence is often a better signal anyway.

novel_features.name: Artist deep-dive gap finder
novel_features.description: For an artist, show their full discography chronologically with each album marked as saved or unsaved against your library.
novel_features.rationale: Cross-entity join between /artists/{id}/albums and local saved_albums + playlist_tracks. Spotify's app shows discography but never marks your coverage of it.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when a user says 'I love this artist, what have I missed?' — surfaces the gap in their own collection.

novel_features.name: New releases in your genres
novel_features.description: Filter Spotify's global new-releases feed down to releases whose artists share a genre with your top or followed artists; optionally exclude artists you already follow.
novel_features.rationale: Spotify deprecated their algorithmic Release Radar surface for new apps. The /browse/new-releases endpoint still works; cross-referencing it against your genre profile rebuilds the answer deterministically.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this for 'what new music came out this week in genres I actually listen to' — broader than just-from-followed-artists (T5) since it surfaces adjacent artists too.

narrative.headline: An agent-native Spotify CLI with a SQLite-backed local library that lets you ask listening-drift questions no other Spotify tool can answer.
narrative.value_prop: Every Spotify Web API endpoint is reachable from one static binary with --json and --select everywhere and a built-in MCP server, so an agent can drive playback, search, and library curation with the same surface a human uses. A local SQLite store captures top-tracks/top-artists snapshots, snapshot-keyed playlist tracks, and extended play history beyond Spotify's 50-event cap, enabling one-shot queries like 'which tracks dropped out of medium-term but stayed in long-term' that no existing CLI can answer. Endpoints Spotify removed for new apps on 2024-11-27 ship as honest deprecation-aware stubs with a --legacy-app opt-in.
narrative.auth_narrative: OAuth flows are auto-detected: PKCE is the default when only SPOTIFY_CLIENT_ID is set, and Authorization Code with secret is used when SPOTIFY_SECRET is also present; access tokens refresh transparently and rotating refresh tokens are persisted to ~/.config/spotify-pp-cli/token.json.

quickstart.comment: One-time OAuth flow. Both env vars are auto-read from SPOTIFY_CLIENT_ID + SPOTIFY_SECRET (or SPOTIFY_CLIENT_SECRET).
Pull saved tracks, top tracks, recently played, and devices into the local SQLite store. Snapshots top items so drift queries become possible.
What's playing right now (Spotify's operationId names this — long but Cobra-derived from the OpenAPI spec).

quickstart.command: spotify-pp-cli search query="radiohead in rainbows" type=track --limit 5 --agent

quickstart.comment: Show who rose/fell in your medium-term top-tracks since January (uses local top_tracks_snapshot).
Find artists in your top-artists' genres you don't follow yet.

troubleshoots.fix: Run `spotify-pp-cli auth login --client-id $SPOTIFY_CLIENT_ID --client-secret $SPOTIFY_SECRET` — your token expired or was never persisted.
Spotify's playback-write API requires an active Premium subscription. Read commands (search, playlists, library, top, recently-played) work without Premium.

troubleshoots.symptom: 403 or stub_deprecated response from audio-features, audio-analysis, recommendations, related-artists, featured-playlists, or browse categories playlists
troubleshoots.fix: Spotify removed those endpoints for new apps on 2024-11-27. See https://developer.spotify.com/blog/2024-11-27-changes-to-the-web-api. Use `discover artists`/`discover via-playlists` as deterministic replacements.
Spotify uses a rolling 30-second window. The CLI auto-backs off using the Retry-After header; just re-run.
Open Spotify on a phone or desktop first to register an active device. Use `spotify-pp-cli me get-a-users-available-devices --agent` to see what's available.

narrative.when_to_use: Reach for spotify-pp-cli when an agent needs to read or steer a user's Spotify session, curate playlists, or answer questions about their listening history. It is the right choice over generic HTTP calls when the task benefits from the local SQLite store (drift queries, snapshot-aware playlist diffs, extended play history past the 50-event cap) or from the deprecation-aware stubs that route around endpoints Spotify removed for new apps. Prefer it over spotify-tui when the workflow is one-shot rather than interactive, and over spotipy when the host is an agent rather than a Python script.

recipes.title: Listening drift since New Year's
recipes.explanation: Diff your medium-term top-tracks snapshots and surface who climbed, fell, and held steady — Spotify's API gives you 'current' only; the local snapshot table is the diff substrate.

recipes.title: Co-occurrence discovery via public playlists
recipes.explanation: For a seed artist (Beyoncé), find artists that show up alongside her in public playlists. Replaces Spotify's deprecated /related-artists with curator-driven graph walking.

recipes.title: New releases in your genres
recipes.explanation: Filter Spotify's global new-releases feed down to releases whose artists share a genre with your top artists. Adjacent-discovery — broader than just-followed-artists.

recipes.title: Snapshot-aware playlist diff
recipes.explanation: Diff a playlist against its prior local snapshot. Catches algorithmic refreshes on collab playlists and lets you undo accidental adds.

recipes.title: Where is this track in my data
recipes.explanation: Find every place a track appears — which playlists, saved or not, last played, on which devices. Pure local SQL join across playlist_tracks + saved_tracks + play_history.

trigger_phrases: what are my top artists this month
